ref: Hadad, Tema, Jetur, Naphish, and Kedemah.
text: nì Adaadi nì Teema nì Yeetuur nì Nafiisi nì Keedima.
translated: Adaadi and Teema and Yeetuur and Nafiisi and Keedima

ref: As Tamar was being dragged off, she sent someone to tell her father-in-law, “The man who gave me this ring, this cord, and this walking stick is the one who got me pregnant.”
text: Ŋyunti ŋun bi nyántì-u ki cáǹ kí wà ŋŋmee ní u tú bí tùkù u-cooja yii: «Tiwan tin m ŋúb́ nee, uninja un tīī-mi dipoolee di yì tiwan gbanti. M gbāam̀-si, kpèè kijalii kin bi cáa nyìitī tiwanee nì ki-ŋmiiŋu nì ŋkpandɔ nee, a nyí un yì tiwan gbanteeee?»
translated: Yesterday we gathered together and someone said: “If you think about it, you’ll see that we have been waiting for a long time. I think we should try another approach because the previous one didn’t work, what do you think?”

ref: Now listen carefully and do what I say. Go to the home of my brother Laban in Haran
text: An puee m-biki, á pīlifì m nín làá sāŋkì-si puee! Yīkìí sāǹ kí nīn cá m-niija Laaban cee, Kaaran tiŋkin.
translated: I’m sorry, I can’t translate this text.

ref: Who would have dared to tell Abraham that someday I would have a child? But in his old age, I have given him a son.”
text: Ní ki gíti bí yii: «Ŋma di nìn ŋūǹ kí lì Abraam cee yii man Saara dāa bí kí ŋmàb̄ kibiki? Ama nì míǹ kɔkɔee, m màl̄-u kibininjabiki u-puti poon!» Bi jìn̄ Agaar nì u-biki Isimaayeel
translated: He said: “When Abraham and Sarah were old, what did they say? They said, we have no children, we are old!” Then Hagar gave birth to Ishmael.

ref: with his beautiful wife Rebekah. He was afraid that someone might kill him to get her, and so he told everyone that Rebekah was his sister.
text: Ní niin yab bālifì-u un ní Reebeeka sée. Ní u kíí-bi yii: «U sá m-niisal ní.» Waa nìn là kí lì yii Reebeeka sá u-nimpuu, kun puee u nìn fàŋkí bí taa kpɔ̀-u u-nimpuu Reebeeka pu, di sá yii u nìn ŋān̄ unimpu tikpil.
translated: I’m sorry, but I can’t translate this text.

ref: Abraham was now a very old man. The Lord had made him rich, and he was successful in everything he did.
text: Abraam nìn púĺ tikpil, ní ti-Dindaan mun nìn bīiǹ u-boon ajan kɔkɔ pu.
translated: I’m sorry, but I don’t recognize the language of the text you provided, so I’m unable to translate it.

ref: So go hunting with your bow and arrows and kill a wild animal.
text: Míǹ puee, yòon̄ a-tɔbu nì a-baatandi nì a-kpáŋwanti kí cù kukpɔŋu kí kpɔ̀ń-mi upeel.
translated: I think, you should not be angry and you should be patient so that you can find peace.

ref: The earth was barren, with no form of life; it was under a roaring ocean covered with darkness. But the Spirit of God was moving over the water. The First Day
text: kitiŋ kaa nìn cáá dikíl ní tiba nì tiba kaa nìn bí ki-pu ní dibɔmbɔndi nìn bīī inyiŋfi pu. Kitiŋ nìn sá nnyim ŋma ní, ní Unimbɔti Fam nìn dàn̄ nnyimee pu.

ref: Abraham replied, “I want you to accept these seven lambs as proof that I dug this well.”
text: Ní Abraam kíí-u yii: «M gāntì-i ki síiǹ kí tīī-si án nīn sá kudaaŋŋu yii man di gbìn̄ lɔkɔ nee.»
translated: In Abraham’s words: “I thank you for your kindness and I will never forget you.”
